ksa_text: Knowledge of data visualization tools and techniques, including chart type selection, visual encoding principles, cognitive load management, accessibility requirements, and the distinction between exploratory and explanatory visualization.

Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Applied Analytics & BI KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.416) to justify inclusion for the VP Data Architecture. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world VP Data Architecture roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Applied Analytics & BI is not a standard competency expectation for VP Data Architecture. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the VP Data Architecture competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.416) and edge-domain placement (Applied Analytics & BI) do not meet inclusion threshold for the VP Data Architecture. All passes support exclusion.
